title: Would a Massachusetts income tax cut actually help you? Here’s what to know.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts voters may consider a ballot question to lower the state income tax rate from 5% to 4%, impacting state revenue.
The proposed tax cut could save the median household approximately $1,250 annually, benefiting higher-income households more significantly.
Experts warn the tax cut could decrease state tax revenue by roughly 10%, affecting funding for core services like education and health.

title: 3 ways Cade Cunningham’s absence could impact the Celtics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cade Cunningham of the Detroit Pistons is out with a collapsed lung, sidelined until at least early April.
Cunningham's absence may affect Detroit's playoff vulnerability, impacting the Celtics' path to the Conference Finals.
Cunningham missing games might boost Jaylen Brown's MVP chances as Brown has played more games than other top competitors.

title: Readers say this is Massachusetts’ most stressful commute — and it’s not I-93 North
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Nearly half of Boston.com readers found the Southeast Expressway the most stressful commute in Massachusetts.
18% of readers cited I-95/Route 128 in Waltham, while the Zakim Bridge/I-93 North received 13% of votes.
Complaints about the Southeast Expressway include constant congestion and being outdated, regardless of the time of day.

title: A grave situation: Medway police seek return of 12-foot-tall skeleton’s head
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Medway Police are investigating the disappearance of a 12-foot-tall skeleton's head from Medway Recreation Center.
The head of "Kevin Skellington" was last seen on Friday and its disappearance is being treated as larceny.
Medway Parks and Recreation is reviewing security footage and asking the public for tips on the missing item.

title: Dropkick Murphys to play at ‘No Kings’ rally on Boston Common
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Dropkick Murphys to perform at 'No Kings' rally on Boston Common on March 28 from 2 to 4 p.m.
The 'No Kings' rally is organized by groups including ACLU of Massachusetts and Indivisible Mass Coalition.
'No Kings Day' features nationwide demonstrations, originating in June 2025 with protests against Trump's policies.

title: Sold out show? Not if you book at this Boston hotel.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Omni Boston Hotel at the Seaport offers All Access Pass for VIP event tickets, including sold-out concerts in 2026.
Winter rates start at $300, with All Access Pass ranging from $1,000 to $1,300 depending on guest budget.
Guests receive overnight stay, event tickets, venue transport, and can add options like sound check access and VIP lounge.

title: Six from N.H. charged in ‘brazen’ Downtown Crossing assault and robbery
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Six New Hampshire residents were charged with unarmed robbery and assault at Downtown Crossing MBTA station.
Victim's phone tracking and surveillance footage helped police find the suspects, some with blood on clothing.
Suspects released on $500 bail; ordered to avoid victim and crime scene; court hearing set for May 19.

title: Review: ‘Project Hail Mary’ borders on miraculous
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Ryan Gosling stars as an amnesiac astronaut in 'Project Hail Mary,' a sci-fi film directed by Phil Lord and Christopher Miller.
The film features Gosling's character teaming up with an alien to stop a crisis caused by sun-dimming organisms known as Astrophage.
'Project Hail Mary' is adapted from a novel by Andy Weir, with the first part praised for its humor but criticized for its lengthy runtime.

title: City Council calls for more transparency from Wu over transportation projects
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council seeks transparency from Mayor Wu on transportation project delays amid her requirement for personal approval.
Councilors Breadon and Durkan sponsored hearing orders addressing concerns over stalled projects worth an estimated $200 million.
Councilors urge understanding of state and federal funding deadlines amid volatile times, with Durkan calling for an updated transportation plan.

title: ‘All the negativity that’s in this town sucks’: Revisiting Rick Pitino’s iconic Celtics rant
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Rick Pitino ranted about Celtics legends not returning to save the team during a press conference on March 1, 2000.
Pitino criticized Boston's negative sports culture and compared it to fans booing Jim Rice and Carl Yastrzemski.
Rick Pitino resigned as Celtics coach on January 8, 2001, with a record of 102-146 over three-and-a-half seasons.
